Literal.Value:
'import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.ticker import FuncFormatter

# Colors
COLORS = {
    "current": "#5B8DBE",
    "py": "#D4A574",
    "gray_700": "#495057",
    "gray_600": "#6C757D",
    "gray_400": "#ADB5BD"
}

# Month order
month_order = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"]

# Sort by month
dataset["month_index"] = dataset["Calendar Month (ie Jan)"].apply(lambda x: month_order.index(x) if x in month_order else -1)
dataset = dataset.sort_values("month_index")

# Get last index
last_idx = len(dataset) - 1

# Create figure with larger size
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5.5, 8.8))

# Plot lines without markers
ax.plot(
    dataset["month_index"],
    dataset["Order Lines YTD"],
    color=COLORS["current"],
    linewidth=3.5,
    label="Order Lines YTD"
)

ax.plot(
    dataset["month_index"],
    dataset["Order Lines (PY) YTD"],
    color=COLORS["py"],
    linewidth=3.5,
    linestyle="--",
    label="Order Lines (PY) YTD"
)

# Add marker ONLY on last point for both lines
ax.plot(
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["month_index"],
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines YTD"],
    marker="o",
    markersize=12,
    markerfacecolor="white",
    markeredgecolor=COLORS["current"],
    markeredgewidth=3.5
)

ax.plot(
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["month_index"],
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines (PY) YTD"],
    marker="o",
    markersize=12,
    markerfacecolor="white",
    markeredgecolor=COLORS["py"],
    markeredgewidth=3.5
)

# Add labels ONLY on last point
def format_k(val):
    return f"{int(round(val/1000))}K"

ax.text(
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["month_index"] + 0.4,
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines YTD"],
    format_k(dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines YTD"]),
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=COLORS["current"],
    va="center"
)

ax.text(
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["month_index"] + 0.4,
    dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines (PY) YTD"],
    format_k(dataset.iloc[last_idx]["Order Lines (PY) YTD"]),
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=COLORS["py"],
    va="center"
)

# X-axis with breathing room for labels
ax.set_xlim(-0.5, 13.5)
ax.set_xticks(range(12))
ax.set_xticklabels(month_order, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=13)

# Y-axis with breathing room
max_val = max(dataset["Order Lines YTD"].max(), dataset["Order Lines (PY) YTD"].max())
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.15)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(FuncFormatter(lambda x, p: f"{int(round(x/1000))}K"))
ax.set_ylabel("Order Lines YTD", fontsize=14, color=COLORS["gray_700"])

# Grid and spines
ax.grid(axis="y", color=COLORS["gray_400"], linewidth=0.8, alpha=0.5)
ax.grid(axis="x", visible=False)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["left"].set_color(COLORS["gray_400"])
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(COLORS["gray_400"])

# Legend
ax.legend(loc="upper left", frameon=False, fontsize=13)

# Styling
ax.tick_params(axis="both", labelsize=13, colors=COLORS["gray_600"], length=0)

# Adjust layout with right margin for labels
fig.subplots_adjust(right=0.85)
plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0, 0.85, 1])
plt.show()'
